skill.name: multi-platform-apps-multi-platform
skill.description: Build and deploy the same feature consistently across web, mobile, and desktop platforms using API-first architecture and parallel implementation strategies.
skill.summary: Orchestrates multiple specialized AI agents to ensure feature parity across web, iOS, Android, and desktop platforms with shared API contracts and design systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 39 static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is a documentation/workflow guide containing no executable code. Pattern matches for 'cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' are triggered by technology terminology (Material Design, MVI, Room database) in documentation text. The backtick at line 29 is Markdown formatting for inline code, not shell execution. No security risks detected.

content.user_title: Deploy features across web, mobile, and desktop platforms
content.value_statement: Building the same feature across multiple platforms creates inconsistencies and duplicate work. This skill orchestrates AI agents to maintain feature parity while optimizing for each platform's conventions.
actual_capabilities: Designs API contracts and OpenAPI specifications before implementation
Creates cross-platform design systems with platform-specific adaptations
Architects shared business logic using TypeScript or Kotlin Multiplatform
Generates platform-specific implementations for React, SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose, and Electron/Tauri
Validates feature parity through automated cross-platform testing matrices
Produces API documentation with SDK examples for each platform
limitations: Requires Task tool with subagent_type capability to orchestrate specialized AI agents
Assumes access to platform-specific development environments and toolchains
Does not execute code directly - produces implementation guidance and specifications
Best suited for greenfield features rather than refactoring existing platform-specific codebases

use_cases.title: Startup launching MVP across all platforms
use_cases.description: A small team needs to launch a new feature on web, iOS, and Android simultaneously to meet investor expectations. This skill coordinates parallel development with shared contracts to achieve feature parity without platform delays.

use_cases.title: Enterprise maintaining cross-platform consistency
use_cases.description: Large organizations with separate web and mobile teams struggle with feature divergence. This workflow establishes API-first design and shared business logic to ensure consistent behavior across all customer touchpoints.

use_cases.title: Agency delivering client projects faster
use_cases.description: Development agencies building custom apps for multiple clients can reuse the parallel implementation pattern to reduce delivery time while maintaining platform-specific quality standards for each client engagement.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Single feature across two platforms
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add a user profile feature to my existing web app and mobile app. Help me design the API contract first, then guide me through implementing it consistently on both platforms using this workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: New product with three platforms
prompt_templates.prompt: We are building a task management app from scratch for web, iOS, and Android. Use the multi-platform workflow to design the API specification, create the design system, architect shared business logic, and generate implementation guidance for all three platforms in parallel.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Full workflow with testing and optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute the complete multi-platform development workflow for a real-time collaboration feature with WebSocket support. Include API design with GraphQL subscriptions, design system with dark mode, shared state management, implementations for Next.js/SwiftUI/Jetpack Compose, cross-platform testing matrix, and platform-specific performance optimizations.

prompt_templates.title: Desktop extension of existing mobile app
prompt_templates.prompt: We have a working iOS and Android app and need to add a desktop version using Tauri. Follow Phase 2d of the workflow to implement desktop with native OS integration, reusing existing API contracts and adapting the shared business logic patterns.

output_examples.input: Design API for a notification feature
output_examples.output: OpenAPI 3.1 specification with REST endpoints for notification CRUD operations, WebSocket event schema for real-time delivery, authentication requirements using bearer tokens, rate limiting at 100 requests per minute, and error response formats with retry-after headers.

output_examples.input: Create design system for cross-platform app
output_examples.output: Component specifications mapping Material Design tokens to iOS Human Interface Guidelines and Fluent Design equivalents, responsive breakpoints for web layouts, native SwiftUI and Compose component implementations, WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility requirements, and dark theme color mappings.

best_practices: Define API contracts and data models before any platform implementation to ensure all teams work from shared specifications
Establish regular synchronization points during parallel development to catch feature divergence early
Use automated cross-platform testing to validate feature parity and catch platform-specific edge cases before release
anti_patterns: Implementing platforms sequentially instead of in parallel, which delays integration issues and increases rework
Creating platform-specific APIs that duplicate functionality instead of using a unified API-first approach
Ignoring platform conventions in favor of identical UI, which creates poor user experiences on each platform

faq.question: What platforms does this workflow support?
faq.answer: The primary platforms are web (React/Next.js), iOS (SwiftUI), Android (Jetpack Compose), and desktop (Electron/Tauri). You can customize the --platforms flag to target specific platforms for your project.

faq.question: Do I need to implement all platforms at once?
faq.answer: No. The workflow is modular - you can run individual phases or target specific platforms. However, the API-first approach works best when you plan for multiple platforms from the start, even if you implement them incrementally.

faq.question: How does this skill handle platform-specific features?
faq.answer: The workflow includes platform abstraction layers and adapter patterns. Core business logic remains shared, while platform-specific features (like Face ID, widgets, or system tray) are implemented as extensions that do not affect the shared contract.

faq.question: Can this work with existing codebases?
faq.answer: Yes, but it works best for greenfield features. For existing codebases, you can apply the API-first design to new features while gradually refactoring existing endpoints to match the unified contract.

faq.question: What level of code reuse can I expect?
faq.answer: With proper API-first design and shared business logic architecture, you can achieve 40-60% code reuse between platforms. Web and desktop implementations share the most code, while native mobile platforms share business logic patterns rather than UI code.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code or generate implementations?
faq.answer: This skill provides implementation guidance, specifications, and architectural patterns through AI agent orchestration. It does not directly execute code or deploy applications - it produces the documentation and code structure for developers to implement.
